title: Legacy Question
html:
A question template Yahir reuses across years and life chapters: "if I died today, was the life worth it?" — surfaced in different forms but with a consistent diagnostic purpose. The earliest specimen is the August 2019 2019 08 12 keep things simple pitch-and-philosophy scratchpad, where the questions sit in a parallel column to the disruption / elevator pitch prompts: "What do you see in the wake of death / What legacy do you want to leave behind / What is the meaning to life, or what is your meaning to life." Founder questions and meaning-of-life questions get drafted side-by-side at age 17 — the template is that old.

The May/June 2024 2024 05 13 things note carries the recurring instruction "write yourself a letter 1 year from now, 3 years from now, 5 years from now, 10 years from now, 20 years from now" — repeated verbatim across multiple date headers, the future-self variant of the same question. The November 2024 2024 11 28 leading thoughts about 6 month adventure manifesto dials it inward: "this life is probably an illusion," paired with soul as beacon and stray dog and lightning bolt identity statements. The cleanest restatement lives in the December 2024 2024 12 14 departing china in-flight reflection: "If I were to die today, my life will have been amazing."

What makes this a template and not just a recurring theme is that Yahir uses it as a check, not a meditation. It shows up at transition points (leaving for college, leaving China, the Austin move) and resolves quickly into action. The 2019 version flows into pitch contacts. The 2024 versions flow into the Brotein / BroMenu sprint and the Austin move logistics. The check passes — amazing — and Yahir keeps building. The question's real function is to make sure that's still the answer.

This is paired with fear of not doing (which arrived in the same 2019 note) — together they're the inertia and the check on it.

Source summaries

  • 2019 08 12 keep things simple — first articulation
  • 2024 05 13 things — letter-to-future-self variant
  • 2024 11 28 leading thoughts about 6 month adventure — identity/illusion variant
  • 2024 12 14 departing china — cleanest restatement
  • first batch 2026 04 26 — meta-tracking the template

Connections

  • fear of not doing — the paired engine
  • — the form it usually arrives in
  • end of chapter reflection — the situation where it deploys
  • letter to future self — explicit variant
  • china chapter — recurring backdrop
  • disruption — co-arrival in the 2019 note
